steps.title: Group the polynomial to expose common factors
steps.content: Rewrite the four-term polynomial as two pairs: $(7x^2 - 9x) + (14x - 18)$. This grouping reveals a common binomial factor.

steps.title: Factor each group separately
steps.content:
$$7x^2 - 9x = x(7x - 9)$$
$$14x - 18 = 2(7x - 9)$$
Both groups now contain $(7x - 9)$ as a factor.

steps.title: Factor out the common binomial
steps.content:
$$x(7x - 9) + 2(7x - 9) = (x + 2)(7x - 9)$$
The complete factorization is $(x + 2)(7x - 9)$. The factors are $(x + 2)$ and $(7x - 9)$. Among the choices, only $(x + 2)$ appears directly. That matches choice D — D is correct.
